findings: Consistent high class separation in 'Average Packet Size' across multiple partitions confirms stable class-conditioned structural variation.
Local evidence from e8, e9, and e10 partitions supports robustness of packet length feature variation without detected drift.
Unresolved tension regarding low cardinality vs. strong class variation remains noted but does not contradict stability findings.
limitations: Analysis limited to 'Average Packet Size' feature due to budget and action constraints; other packet length features not independently verified in this task.
